payload.text: fsguard read-before-write guard behavior is covered by a green -race test suite
fsguard.CheckWritable derives freshness from a content hash of the current on-disk bytes (hashBytes(current)), not a timestamp
Connect the guard to a concrete file-mutating tool: MarkRead after Read, CheckWritable/CommitWrite before write, MarkWritten after. First real consumer of pkg/tools/fsguard.
payload.title: Wire fsguard into a Write/Edit agent tool

payload.text: fsguard enforces freshness only; it does not bound where writes land. Add a peer that refuses writes resolving outside the workspace root (../ escapes, symlink-to-$HOME, .git/hooks). Per ADR-007 boundary.
payload.title: Add workspace path-scoping sibling (bound write destinations)

payload.text: Partial reads (offset/limit) currently authorize edits anywhere in the file. Track observed ranges and verify the edited span falls within one, closing the provenance-overstatement gap.
payload.title: Add edit-region / read-range coverage to fsguard

payload.text: CommitWrite narrows but does not close the external check-vs-write window. Investigate OS atomic primitives (O_EXCL dance / rename swap / compare-and-swap on hash) to make verify-and-write atomic against out-of-process writers.
payload.title: Close out-of-process TOCTOU in CommitWrite

payload.text: read_file/write_file (pkg/tools/file) route mutations through fsguard.CommitWrite; an unread overwrite is refused (ErrNotRead) — covered by a green -race suite

payload.basis: Added pkg/tools/file: read_file + write_file sharing one fsguard.Guard; write routes through CommitWrite

payload.text: pkg/tools/pathscope refuses reads/writes resolving outside the workspace root incl. ..-traversal, absolute-path and symlink escapes; wired into read_file/write_file via file.NewOSInWorkspace; covered by a green -race suite

payload.basis: Built pkg/tools/pathscope (Scope.Check over canonicalized paths, minimal Canonicalizer seam reusing fsguard.OSFilesystem, static ErrOutsideWorkspace), wired scope checks into pkg/tools/file read_file/write_file via NewOSInWorkspace before any fs/guard access; ADR-008 accepted; commit 09ac645

payload.text: fsguard refuses an edit whose line span was never observed by a Read (CheckEditable/ErrRegionNotRead per ADR-009); covered by tests, additive to ADR-007 semantics

payload.basis: TDD on pkg/tools/fsguard: 25 new tests first (RED via not-implemented stubs), then MarkReadRange/CheckEditable/ErrRegionNotRead implemented to green; Snapshot extended additively with legacy-JSON back-compat test; ADR-009 records line-vs-byte decision; committed 3f318c8

payload.text: fsguard.CommitWrite now refuses a new-file create raced by an external create (kernel-enforced O_EXCL, static sentinel ErrConcurrentCreate, external file preserved) and publishes existing-file overwrites via write-temp-then-rename so a concurrent reader never observes a torn write, re-verifying the content hash immediately before the write call; the out-of-process TOCTOU window on existing files is narrowed, NOT closed — an external modification landing between that final re-read and the rename is still overwritten, per ADR-010 Residual risk. Covered by injected-race and rename-atomicity tests in pkg/tools/fsguard under -race.

payload.basis: Replaced the fsguard Filesystem seam's WriteFile with CreateExclusive (O_CREATE|O_EXCL) and WriteFileAtomic (temp+fsync+rename); rebuilt CommitWrite on them with the freshness re-read immediately before the write; added ErrConcurrentCreate; race simulated via injectable fakeFS writeHook plus OS-level rename-atomicity tests; shipped in commit af57f91 with ADR-010 and CHANGELOG entry

payload.text: base runtime interpolates tool results verbatim into model-facing messages via fmt.Sprintf("%v", result) in internal/runtime/base/runtime.go

evidence.command: grep -rn 'Tool execution failed: ' internal/runtime --include='*.go' | wc -l | tr -d ' '

payload.text: both runtime loops feed raw err.Error() to the model as 'Tool execution failed: '+err at 5 sites across internal/runtime (local + temporal)

the agent loop is implemented twice with mirrored structure: LocalRuntime.RunAgentLoop (local/agent_loop.go) and TemporalRuntime.AgentWorkflow (temporal/agent_workflow.go); retry semantics already diverge (local aborts on LLM error, temporal retries)
no recover() exists anywhere under internal/runtime or pkg/agent; local runtime runs tools in bare goroutines, so a panicking tool crashes the process on the local path

agent_workflow.go calls uuid.New() for messageID directly inside the workflow iteration loop, not wrapped in workflow.SideEffect (unlike its two SideEffect-wrapped siblings) — a replay-nondeterminism smell

evidence.command: grep -rl 'pkg/tools/fsguard\|pkg/tools/file"\|pkg/tools/pathscope' --include='*.go' pkg internal examples | grep -v '^pkg/tools/' | wc -l | tr -d ' '

payload.text: the fsguard/file/pathscope safety trio is imported by nothing outside pkg/tools and registered in no example — library-only, unwired into the agent runtime

Store message references (not transcripts) in workflow state; document+tool a versioning discipline for in-flight agent workflows; add an evaluator-optimizer gate primitive and a typed pipeline-step API over activities. Per ADR-011 phase 3; adopts PAT entries from docs/evaluations/patterns-to-adopt.md.

payload.text: Extend TokenBudget to spend-based per-run budgets with kill-switch; add an eval/regression harness seam; document Temporal event history as the first-class audit artifact. Per ADR-011 phase 4.

payload.text: Declare Local the dev-mode of the single production loop. Per ADR-011 phase 2.

payload.text: Wrap tool results in a typed delimited envelope; static model-facing error text (raw error harness-only); defer recover() + per-tool timeout in local tool execution; wire fsguard/pathscope + Snapshot into the runtime; wrap agent_workflow.go messageID uuid in workflow.SideEffect. Per ADR-011 phase 1.

evidence.command: go test -tags escape_repro -run TestScopedWrite ./pkg/tools/file/ 2>&1 | grep -q 'ESCAPE CONFIRMED' && echo VULN

payload.text: pathscope workspace bound is bypassable: a scoped write to a raw symlink-then-dotdot path ('<root>/link/../x', link->outside) is accepted and bytes land OUTSIDE root; Check cleans '..' lexically while CommitWrite writes the original un-cleaned path via os.OpenFile resolved through the symlink

Close the confirmed escape (tr-8f969e5d). Check judges a lexically-cleaned path; the write uses the raw OS path resolved through symlinks. Fix: Check must validate the path the write will actually use (symlink-resolved), and/or the write path must refuse symlink-traversing targets (openat2 RESOLVE_BENEATH / lstat-walk; O_NOFOLLOW alone covers only the final component). Then move escape_repro_test.go into the normal suite as a passing regression guard. Part of ADR-011 phase-1 membrane, but a standalone security P0.
payload.title: Fix pathscope symlink+dotdot workspace escape (P0 security)

evidence.command: grep -Eqn 'CreateExclusive\(path,' pkg/tools/fsguard/fsguard.go && grep -Eqn 'WriteFileAtomic\(path,' pkg/tools/fsguard/fsguard.go && echo RAWPATH-IO

payload.text: fsguard.CommitWrite computes its freshness key from Canonical(path) (fsguard.go:351) but performs ReadFile/CreateExclusive/WriteFileAtomic on the raw path (358/363/384), so under symlink layouts the freshness key and the on-disk write target can refer to different files — the write-side mechanism behind the pathscope escape tr-8f969e5d; not exercised by the suite

payload.text: the scoped-write workspace escape (lexical '..' vs kernel symlink resolution) is closed on BOTH surfaces (pathscope Check + fsguard write path); sibling/upward/intermediate-symlink '..' escapes all refuse with ErrOutsideWorkspace and land zero bytes outside root, guarded by escape_repro_test.go now in the normal suite; residuals NOT closed: ADR-010 TOCTOU + symlink-swap-between-resolve-and-open (no O_NOFOLLOW/openat2), and unscoped file.New/NewOS bundles by design

payload.basis: Fable5 agent fixed the workspace escape at commit 5586595: OSFilesystem.Canonical (shared by pathscope.Scope + fsguard.Guard) now resolves paths component-by-component in kernel order (symlinks before '..', ELOOP-bounded, fail-closed), and fsguard.Guard does all I/O on the canonical path not the raw path — approved path == written path. Independently verified: 4 escape variants refuse, in-root symlink control allowed, pkg/tools -race green, build OK.
